# Copilot Instructions

## Repository purpose

This repository is a private knowledge base for side-job operations in Japan.
It stores practical notes about administration, bookkeeping, tax preparation, and reusable checklists for someone who is new to side work.

## Authoring rules

- Prefer Markdown over code.
- Write document content in Japanese unless the user asks otherwise.
- Use ASCII kebab-case for file and directory names.
- Keep one topic per Markdown file.
- Put long-form guidance under docs/ and use assets/ only for supporting files such as images and PDFs.
- If you add a PDF or image, also add or update a Markdown file that explains why the asset exists and what matters in it.

## Content quality rules

- Treat tax and legal topics as time-sensitive.
- When documenting tax or制度 topics, include the applicable year, last checked date, and source link when possible.
- Prefer checklists, decision points, and concrete operational steps over abstract summaries.
- Do not invent legal certainty. If a rule depends on conditions, say so explicitly.

## Privacy and safety rules

- Never add personal information, account credentials, API keys, My Number, or real contract and invoice originals.
- Avoid storing screenshots or files that expose client names, amounts, addresses, or filing details.
- If an example is needed, use anonymized or fully redacted examples only.

## Maintenance rules

- Keep README.md aligned with the current repository structure.
- Follow the structure and conventions documented in docs/repository-structure.md.
- When reorganizing documents, preserve discoverability and update links.
